## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Napoleonic_Wars&action=edit&section=32 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-1)** [Dissolved in 1806](/wiki/Dissolution_of_the_Holy_Roman_Empire "Dissolution of the Holy Roman Empire"). Many states of the Empire were allies of France before its dissolution, including [Bavaria](/wiki/Electorate_of_Bavaria "Electorate of Bavaria"), [Saxony](/wiki/Electorate_of_Saxony "Electorate of Saxony"), [Baden](/wiki/Electorate_of_Baden "Electorate of Baden") and [Württemberg](/wiki/Electorate_of_W%C3%BCrttemberg "Electorate of Württemberg").
2. **[^](#cite_ref-2)** Hanover was in [personal union with the United Kingdom](/wiki/Personal_union_of_Great_Britain_and_Hanover "Personal union of Great Britain and Hanover"). It was annexed by Westphalia in 1806 and restored as the [Kingdom of Hanover](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Hanover "Kingdom of Hanover") in 1814. Hanover contributed troops to the British Army (see [King's German Legion](/wiki/King%27s_German_Legion "King's German Legion")) and to Wellington's army during the [Waterloo Campaign](/wiki/Waterloo_Campaign "Waterloo Campaign").
3. **[^](#cite_ref-3)** The term *Austrian Empire* came into use after Napoleon crowned himself [Emperor of the French](/wiki/Emperor_of_the_French "Emperor of the French") in 1804, by which [Francis II, Holy Roman Emperor](/wiki/Francis_II,_Holy_Roman_Emperor "Francis II, Holy Roman Emperor") took the title [Emperor of Austria](/wiki/Emperor_of_Austria "Emperor of Austria") (Kaiser von Österreich) in response. The Holy Roman Empire was dissolved in 1806, and consequently *Emperor of Austria* became Francis' primary title. For this reason, *Austrian Empire* is often used instead of *Holy Roman Empire* for brevity's sake when speaking of the Napoleonic Wars, even though the two entities are not synonymous.
4. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-Austria&Prussia_4-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-Austria&Prussia_4-1) [***c***](#cite_ref-Austria&Prussia_4-2) [***d***](#cite_ref-Austria&Prussia_4-3) After the French victory against the [Fourth](/wiki/War_of_the_Fourth_Coalition "War of the Fourth Coalition") and [Fifth](/wiki/War_of_the_Fifth_Coalition "War of the Fifth Coalition") coalitions, Prussia and Austria became allies of France and contributed forces to the [French Invasion of Russia](/wiki/French_Invasion_of_Russia "French Invasion of Russia") in 1812. Both countries joined the [Sixth Coalition](/wiki/War_of_the_Sixth_Coalition "War of the Sixth Coalition") when the invasion failed.
5. **[^](#cite_ref-8)** The [Kingdom of Hungary](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Hungary_(1538%E2%80%931867) "Kingdom of Hungary (1538–1867)") participated in the war with separate Hungarian regiments[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEArnold1995[httpsbooksgooglecombooksid63xTTaYTbrwCpgPA36_36]-5)[[2]](#cite_note-6) in the Imperial and Royal Army, and also by a traditional army ("insurrectio").[[3]](#cite_note-7) The Hungarian Diet voted to join in war and agreed to pay one third of the war expenses.
6. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-Russia_9-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-Russia_9-1) Russia was a leading member of the Coalition, but became an ally of France from the signing of the [Treaties of Tilsit](/wiki/Treaties_of_Tilsit "Treaties of Tilsit") in 1807 until it was [invaded by France](/wiki/French_invasion_of_Russia "French invasion of Russia") in 1812. During this time, it was nominally at war with [the United Kingdom](/wiki/Anglo-Russian_War_(1807%E2%80%931812) "Anglo-Russian War (1807–1812)"), [Iran](/wiki/Russo-Persian_War_(1804%E2%80%931813) "Russo-Persian War (1804–1813)"), [Sweden](/wiki/Finnish_War "Finnish War") and [the Ottoman Empire](/wiki/Russo-Turkish_War_(1806%E2%80%931812) "Russo-Turkish War (1806–1812)").
7. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-Spain_10-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-Spain_10-1) Spain was an ally of France in the [War of the Third Coalition](/wiki/War_of_the_Third_Coalition "War of the Third Coalition") and the [invasion of Portugal](/wiki/Invasion_of_Portugal_(1807) "Invasion of Portugal (1807)"). In 1808, France invaded Spain, triggering the [Peninsular War](/wiki/Peninsular_War "Peninsular War") (or the Spanish War of Independence) and Spain joined the Coalition against France.
8. **[^](#cite_ref-Portugal_11-0)** Portugal became an active belligerent in [1807](/wiki/Invasion_of_Portugal_(1807) "Invasion of Portugal (1807)") following a Franco-Spanish invasion. While a member of the coalition, the regular Portuguese army operated mostly under [British command](/wiki/Anglo-Portuguese_Army "Anglo-Portuguese Army"), who also heavily subsidized Portugal's military.
9. **[^](#cite_ref-Sweden_12-0)** When Russia allied with France, it [conquered Finland](/wiki/Finnish_War "Finnish War"), which was under Swedish rule at the time. After [Marshal Bernadotte](/wiki/Charles_XIV_John "Charles XIV John") became Crown Prince of Sweden in 1810, Sweden nominally declared [war on the United Kingdom](/wiki/Anglo-Swedish_war_of_1810%E2%80%931812 "Anglo-Swedish war of 1810–1812"), but no actual fighting was done. Sweden became a leading member of the [Sixth Coalition](/wiki/War_of_the_Sixth_Coalition "War of the Sixth Coalition") and declared war on France in 1812.
10. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-Seventh_Coalition_13-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-Seventh_Coalition_13-1) [***c***](#cite_ref-Seventh_Coalition_13-2) [***d***](#cite_ref-Seventh_Coalition_13-3) [***e***](#cite_ref-Seventh_Coalition_13-4) [***f***](#cite_ref-Seventh_Coalition_13-5) 1815.
11. **[^](#cite_ref-14)** Until 1806.
12. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-Iran_15-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-Iran_15-1) The Iranian Qajar Dynasty [fought a war against Russia](/wiki/Russo-Persian_War_(1804%E2%80%931813) "Russo-Persian War (1804–1813)") from 1804 to 1813. At the start of the war, Iran received support from France. When [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia") became an ally of France in 1807, French support for Iran ended, and Iran was instead supported by the United Kingdom.
13. **[^](#cite_ref-16)** 1806–1807, 1813–1814
14. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-German_States_1_17-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-German_States_1_17-1) [***c***](#cite_ref-German_States_1_17-2) [***d***](#cite_ref-German_States_1_17-3) From 1813.
15. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-Denmark_18-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-Denmark_18-1) After the [Second Battle of Copenhagen](/wiki/Second_Battle_of_Copenhagen "Second Battle of Copenhagen") in 1807, Denmark-Norway became an ally of France. After [losing Norway to Sweden](/wiki/Dano-Swedish_War_(1813-1814) "Dano-Swedish War (1813-1814)") in 1814, Denmark switched sides and joined the Coalition.
16. **[^](#cite_ref-19)** Nominally only. Ferdinand was proclaimed King of Spain by the [Supreme Central Junta](/wiki/Supreme_Central_Junta "Supreme Central Junta") in 1808, but was effectively imprisoned in France for the duration of the [Peninsular War](/wiki/Peninsular_War "Peninsular War"). The Central Junta, and later the [Cortes of Cádiz](/wiki/Cortes_of_C%C3%A1diz "Cortes of Cádiz"), governed the country in the king's absence until he was allowed to return to Spain under the [Treaty of Valençay](/wiki/Treaty_of_Valen%C3%A7ay "Treaty of Valençay") in 1814.
17. **[^](#cite_ref-20)** [King of Naples](/wiki/King_of_Naples "King of Naples") from 1806 to 1808. [King of Spain](/wiki/King_of_Spain "King of Spain") from 1808 to 1813.
18. **[^](#cite_ref-25)** 250,000 in the British Army and 130,000 in the Royal Navy. The Royal Navy averaged 110,000 personnel during the conflict. The British Army was still 211,276 strong after the conclusion of the conflict in December 1815.[[7]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEClodfelter2017170-24)
19. **[^](#cite_ref-28)** 793,110 in the British Army and 250,000 in the Royal Navy from 1793 to 1815. Casualties for the whole 1793-1815 period are as follows. Army losses: 219,420 dead (25,569 killed in battle or died of wounds, 193,851 dead of disease or other accidents), 86,390 wounded, 20,000 captured, 113,273 deserters. Navy losses: 92,386 dead (6,663 killed in battle; 13,621 drowned or killed in noncombat shipwrecks or fires; 72,102 dead of disease or other accidents), 14,270 wounded.[[9]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEClodfelter2017170–171-27)
20. **[^](#cite_ref-33)** 50,000 of which being guerillas.[[13]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEClodfelter2017152-32)
21. **[^](#cite_ref-94)** Equivalent to £3.5 trillion as proportion of UK GDP in 2016.[[73]](#cite_note-93)
22. **[^](#cite_ref-178)** Many historians say it was not the "first" total war; for a critique of Bell see Frederick C. Schneid (2012).
